page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 36 physical cores and 72 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and includes 320 MB of L3 cache alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for deep learning and matrix operations. Memory capacity stands at 576.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.

A primary differentiator of this instance is its massive local storage configuration, which bundles 45,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity directly with the compute resources. This high-density local storage is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. The instance does not include any dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on its Intel Xeon processor for compute workloads.

Benchmark data indicates strong to top-tier performance across multiple domains. In Passmark database operations, Geekbench image processing, and static web serving throughput, the i7ie.18xlarge achieves top-tier results in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations fall within the strong top 25% tier. However, single-core stress-ng performance and text generation speeds for large 70B language models are average, placing in the middle 50%, highlighting a tradeoff where multi-threaded and memory-bound operations are prioritized over single-threaded raw speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory relative to the 72 vCPUs provides a consolidated hardware profile that reduces the need for external network-attached storage. This makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.18xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 576.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a massive 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no integrated GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and image processing, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. While single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation remain average, the high-density local storage and memory configuration offer significant qualitative cost efficiency by eliminating external storage overhead. This server is engineered for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 72 vCPUs, 576 GB DDR5 RAM, and 45 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.18xlarge features 72 vCPUs, 576 GB DDR5 RAM, and 45 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring forty-five terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity and seventy-two dedicated virtual CPUs.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor at 3.6 GHz
45,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
576.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise applications
